summary:
Claim: Carbon contamination in phosphate-bonded investment causes black castings.  
Evidence: - Carbon reacts with molten metals (e.g., stainless steel) forming non-metallic carbide inclusions. - Carbides disrupt metallurgical bonding, leading to visible surface discoloration (blackening).  
Answer: B
formatted_summary:

Evidence: - Carbon reacts with molten metals (e.g., stainless steel) forming non-metallic carbide inclusions. - Carbides disrupt metallurgical bonding, leading to visible surface discoloration (blackening).  
Answer: B. Answer choice: B
